data.title: Abeona Therapeutics targets $2B revenue potential for pz-cel amid U.S. launch preparations
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Abeona Therapeutics (ABEO) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Vish Seshadri highlighted the upcoming FDA decision on prademagene zamikeracel (pz-cel), scheduled for April 29, 2025, as a pivotal milestone. The company is preparing for a potential launch in Q3 2025, with expectations that pz-cel will become a significant driver of growth and profitability.
  • Seshadri stated that the FDA has provided a marked-up draft label for pz-cel and has not required additional site inspections. Launch preparations include onboarding five renowned EB treatment centers to be qualified treatment centers (QTCs).
  • Chief Commercial Officer Madhav Vasanthavada projected that pz-cel could generate over $2 billion in cumulative revenue in the U.S., based on an estimated 1,500 treatment opportunities for the 750 eligible RDEB patients.
  • CFO Joe Vazzano disclosed that Abeona had $98.1 million in cash and equivalents as of December 31, 2024, providing financial resources to fund operations into 2026 without considering potential pz-cel revenues or a Priority Review Voucher (PRV) sale.

Outlook

  • Management anticipates pz-cel's commercial launch in Q3 2025, pending FDA approval. Five treatment centers are expected to be activated within three months post-approval.
  • Vasanthavada emphasized market readiness, stating that commercial payers covering 60%-65% of RDEB patients have shown positive feedback on pz-cel's clinical value. The company plans to scale manufacturing capacity to address demand.

Financial Results

  • Abeona reported a full-year net loss of $63.7 million for 2024, compared to $54.2 million in 2023. Research and development expenses increased to $34.4 million, primarily due to commercial launch preparation costs.
  • General and administrative expenses rose to $29.9 million, reflecting investments in building a commercial team and other launch preparations.

Q&A

  • Kristen Kluska, Cantor: Asked about FDA satisfaction regarding the resolution of CMC-related issues. Seshadri stated that the company has addressed all items from the CRL and believes it is in a strong position.
  • François Brisebois, Oppenheimer: Asked about physician education and patient community awareness. Vasanthavada noted efforts to onboard key treatment centers and leverage patient advocacy groups for awareness campaigns.
  • Maury Raycroft, Jefferies: Inquired about initial patient treatment volumes and manufacturing capacity. Seshadri confirmed the initial manufacturing capacity at launch would support up to four treatments per month, scaling to 10 treatments per month by 2026.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism about FDA approval and pz-cel's market potential. Questions primarily focused on the regulatory process, manufacturing capacity, and commercial strategy.
  • Management maintained a confident and forward-looking tone, highlighting progress in addressing regulatory requirements and preparing for a successful product launch.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter's focus shifted to commercial readiness and revenue potential for pz-cel, compared to the previous quarter's emphasis on regulatory progress.
  • Management's tone remained confident, with increased detail on market access strategies and manufacturing scale-up plans.
  • Analysts' questions evolved from regulatory concerns to operational execution and market dynamics.

Risks and Concerns

  • Key risks include FDA approval dependency, manufacturing scale-up challenges, and potential delays in treatment center onboarding.
  • Management highlighted mitigation strategies, including early engagement with treatment centers and proactive payer education to ensure reimbursement readiness.

Final Takeaway

Abeona Therapeutics is positioning itself for a transformative 2025, with pz-cel expected to drive significant growth upon potential FDA approval. The company's strategic focus on market preparation, manufacturing capacity expansion, and payer engagement underpins its confidence in capturing a substantial share of the RDEB treatment market.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Freeport McMoRan raised at J.P. Morgan, as tariff risk seen maintaining premium pricing
data.description:
Freeport McMoRan (NYSE:FCX) +0.8% in Thursday's trading as J.P. Morgan upgrades shares to Overweight from Neutral with a $52 price target, raised from $48 previously, saying tariff risk likely will maintain premium pricing for the company's U.S.-based footprint for the foreseeable future, combined with expected long-term supply challenges supporting a favorable through-cycle pricing environment for the industry.

Since President Trump said on February 25 that the Commerce Department would review whether U.S. copper imports pose a national security risk, Comex copper has averaged a 10% premium to LME copper, which should directly benefit Freeport's (NYSE:FCX) exposure, amounting to ~10% of FY 2024 operating profit and an even greater 20% of post-tax earnings given NOLs.

JPM analyst Bill Peterson believes Trump 2.0 trade policy to date infers it is more likely than not that some form of protectionism for U.S. copper imports unfolds, but if not, Freeport (FCX) is "still advantageously positioned relative to pure-play peers to capture incremental upside in the near term," with 2025 mine supply growth forecast decelerating to 1% from 3% last year.

Over the longer term, Peterson sees energy transition demand and lagging mine supply deepening current deficits and supporting higher through-the-cycle copper pricing; taken together with easing capex next year, the analyst sees Freeport (FCX) generating a significant amount of additional free cash flow, which can support the company’s base and performance-
based payout framework.

data.title: SA Asks: What's next for EV automaker BYD?
data.description:
EV maker BYD (OTCPK:BYDDF) has been on a roll.

Earlier this week, the Chinese company made headlines by unveiling a five-minute charging system that can fuel an EV in about the same time as it would take to fill a traditional car with gas. The news came just a few weeks after BYD (OTCPK:BYDDF) announced plans to make its God’s Eye driver-assistance system available in most of its vehicles at no extra charge.

Year-to-date, BYD (OTCPK:BYDDF) shares have risen 60%, compared with the S&P 500, which has slipped 3.4%, as of Thursday afternoon. 

So what's next for BYD (OTCPK:BYDDF)? Seeking Alpha analysts The Techie and Danil Sereda gave us their thoughts on what's ahead for the carmaker. 

The Techie: BYD (OTCPK:BYDDF) not only holds pricing power against Tesla (TSLA), Nio (NIO), Li Auto (LI), and XPeng (XPEV), but it’s also consistently reminding us that it holds the edge in innovation, which isn’t just bound to cars.  

On March 17th, BYD (BYDFF) released the 10C megawatt charging station and the world’s first charger with 1000V and 1000A that can charge 400 km in five minutes. The 1000 kW charger, called the Megawatt Flash Charger, proves rivals are lagging behind, with Tesla (TSLA) V4 at 500 kW, Li Auto (LI)  5C at 520 kW, Nio (NIO) Power at 640 kW and XPeng (XPEV) S5 at 800 kW.  

The company has more than doubled its market cap since 2023, and I think we could see it grow two-fold over the next five years, especially with its advantage of economies of scale.  

BYD’s (OTCPK:BYDDF) global expansion efforts aren’t exempt from EU tariffs, at 27.4% now, or delays in Mexico's plant approval. Still, BYD (OTCPK:BYDDF) stands out from Tesla (TSLA) and the allies by offering innovation at a lower price point. On the other hand, it stands out from the Chinese bunch by being the most affordable with a consistent supply of new products. I think it's the best positioned to mitigate the risks. 

Danil Sereda:  Generally speaking, I'm neutral about the stock if we consider it a potential buying opportunity because of the elevated valuation levels. Of course, BYD (OTCPK:BYDDF) is too far from Tesla (TSLA) in this regard, but still - paying 25x+ forwarding earnings for a Chinese stock is a bit too much for me.  

The company itself is doing a fantastic job at business expansion amid strict cost management that helps it save a ton of money where others fail, including global rival Tesla (TSLA). Local competitors such as Li (LI), Xpeng (XPEV) and Nio (NIO) seem to be lagging behind in terms of selling volumes, pricing power and technology.

BYD's (OTCPK:BYDDF) recent announcement of ultra-fast charging, although it's likely a long process for wide usage, gives them yet another edge in the market, where the vertical integration of their business structure allows them to keep prices relatively low.  

With five-minute charging, EV adoption in China and nearby countries will likely accelerate even further, and so BYD's (OTCPK:BYDDF) earnings growth visibility has now become clearer, in my view. Hence, the valuation premium. But again, I'd wait for a better price.

  • Top Automobile Manufacturers Stocks
  • Top Automotive Retail Stocks

data.title: JPMorgan Chase is the 'Nvidia of banking,' Wells Fargo's Mayo says
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) is outpacing its banking peers in AI adoption, Wells Fargo's Mike Mayo wrote in a recent note, calling it the "Nvidia (NVDA) of banking."

Indeed, the U.S.'s largest lender set aside $17.0B for its 2024 tech budget, easily topping a list of 13 other big banks. Mayo pointed out, citing company reports and Wells Fargo Securities estimates. As a percent of revenue, though, BNY Mellon (BK) came on top at 20%.

"AI integration is becoming more of a necessity and companies that fail to adopt AI will struggle vs AI-powered competitors," the Wednesday note said. "Thus, firms should at least implement AI to improve controls and efficiencies in cyber, fraud, risk & governance."

Early in the month, JPMorgan (NYSE:JPM) and Starwood Property Trust (STWD) agreed to lend $2B for a 100-acre AI data center being built in Utah, one of the largest construction loans in recent years, underscoring the Jamie Dimon-led bank's confidence in the space.

AI use cases are still largely centered on internal efficiencies, Mayo noted, "though benefits should eventually move to work streams and all bank devices, people, and transactions that aid productivity."

In all, he added, AI is expected to boost banks' tech-fueled efficiency ratios, though the benefits might unfold more gradually.

Last year, JPMorgan (JPM) reportedly gave asset and wealth management employees access to its ChatGPT-like large language model that can do the work of a research analyst.

data.title: Team, inc. targets 15% EBITDA growth for 2025 with cost savings and operational improvements
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Team, Inc. (TISI) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Keith Tucker highlighted the successful execution of Team's strategic roadmap over the past two years, which has led to improved financial performance, including expanded margins, increased adjusted EBITDA, and significant cost reductions. He emphasized the refinancing of the capital structure in March 2025, which reduced the blended interest rate by over 100 basis points and extended term loan maturities to 2030.

  • Fourth-quarter results included positive free cash flow and year-over-year improvements in operating income and adjusted EBITDA. Operating income for both business segments increased by 45.4% and 51%, driven by higher-margin opportunities and sustainable cost reductions.

  • Tucker announced that a new series of initiatives aimed at achieving $10 million in annualized cost savings has been launched. These actions, along with improvements to Canadian operations, are expected to positively impact results in 2025, with full benefits realized in 2026.

  • CFO Nelson Haight detailed the refinancing completed in March 2025, which involved a new $175 million first-lien term loan maturing in 2030 and a $50 million delayed draw term loan. Proceeds were used to repay $157.7 million in outstanding debt, lowering near-term maturities and improving financial flexibility.

  • Haight reported that adjusted EBITDA for 2024 reached $54.3 million, a 28% year-over-year increase, despite a 1% decline in revenue. He attributed this to the company's cost-cutting initiatives and focus on higher-margin opportunities, resulting in a significantly improved adjusted EBITDA margin of 6.4% for the year.

Outlook

  • Management projects at least 15% adjusted EBITDA growth for 2025, supported by mid-single-digit revenue growth and improved Canadian operations. Progress toward achieving an adjusted EBITDA margin of 10% remains a priority.

  • CEO Tucker stated that the company continues to experience healthy activity levels across its segments and expects further improvements in margin and financial performance in 2025. The focus will remain on top-line growth initiatives and strict cost discipline.

Financial Results

  • Fourth-quarter adjusted EBITDA improved to $14.6 million, up nearly $5 million year-over-year. Full-year adjusted EBITDA reached $54.3 million, reflecting a 28% increase over 2023.

  • Net loss for 2024 decreased by 50% to $38.3 million, a $37.5 million improvement compared to the prior year. Selling, general, and administrative expenses for the year were reduced by $11 million.

  • Free cash flow generation for 2024 totaled $13.3 million, supported by reduced costs and improved operational execution.

Q&A

  • NOT_APPLICABLE: The transcript did not include a Q&A session.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts exhibited a neutral sentiment as no Q&A session was available to gauge concerns or tone.

  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing achievements in cost reduction, refinancing, and strategic execution. Statements such as "we are well-positioned to sustainably and profitably grow Team well into the future" reflect optimism.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone remained consistent, with continued focus on operational improvements and financial discipline.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Adjusted EBITDA improved sequentially to $14.6 million in Q4 2024 from $11.3 million in Q3 2024. Cost-saving measures and higher-margin opportunities were key contributors to the improvement.

  • Guidance language evolved with a more specific projection of at least 15% adjusted EBITDA growth for 2025, compared to general optimism expressed in Q3 2024.

  • Analysts' focus was not discernible due to the absence of a Q&A session.

  • Management's confidence in achieving its strategic goals remained strong, with continuous emphasis on cost discipline and top-line growth.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged ongoing challenges in the Canadian operations but outlined specific improvement initiatives targeting enhanced performance in 2025.

  • The reliance on cost-saving measures and refinancing to improve financial results could pose risks if these initiatives fail to deliver the expected benefits.

Final Takeaway

Team, Inc. delivered significant operational and financial improvements in 2024, driven by strategic cost reductions and a focus on higher-margin opportunities. With a strengthened capital structure and targeted cost savings of $10 million, the company is poised for at least 15% adjusted EBITDA growth in 2025. Management remains confident in achieving its strategic objectives, backed by a clear roadmap for sustainable and profitable growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Quantum stocks head south even as Nvidia hosts first ever quantum day at GTC
data.description:
Quantum computing stocks tumbled on Thursday, even as Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) held its first-ever Quantum Day at its annual GTC event. 

Leading the way down was D-Wave Quantum (NYSE:QBTS), which fell 18.5% in late afternoon trading. Despite Thursday's sharp decline, shares are up 12% over the past five trading sessions and 17% over the past month, notable when the broader market has come under pressure.

Also seeing heavy losses were Quantum Computing (NASDAQ:QUBT), down 12.5%, Rigetti Computing (RGTI), down 11%, and IonQ (IONQ), which reversed earlier gains and fell more than 9%.

IonQ and Ansys (ANSS) said on Thursday that quantum computing outperformed classical computing when designing key life-saving medical devices.

The declines come as Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang recanted his recent comments about quantum computing being many years away. However, the 62-year-old Huang added he was surprised there were several publicly traded companies focused on the technology.

“This is the first event in history where a company CEO invites all of the guests to explain why he was wrong,” Huang said at the event.

“How could a quantum computer company be public?” Huang added, pointing out that the technology is "insanely complicated."

data.title: Baker Hughes wins technology contract with Petrobras for pipeline corrosion solution
data.description:
Baker Hughes (NASDAQ:BKR) said Thursday it was awarded a major, multi-year fully integrated completions systems contract with Petrobras (PBR) to solve stress corrosion cracking due to carbon dioxide in flexible pipe systems; financial terms were not disclosed.

The pre-commercial agreement includes development and testing, along with an option to purchase the next-generation flexible pipes that will offer a prolonged service life of 30 years in high-CO2 conditions, Baker Hughes (NASDAQ:BKR) said.

SCC-CO2 - stress corrosion cracking due to carbon dioxide - can impact flexible pipes in Brazil's pre-salt fields, which contain high levels of naturally occurring CO2, and when water enters a pipe's annulus area, it can lead to corrosion of the steel reinforcement layers, compromising structural integrity and shortening the system's lifespan, the company said.

Baker Hughes (BKR) "has led the way in addressing SCC-CO2, and we will bring that expertise and experience to bear in developing the definitive solution to this critical industry challenge," said the company's executive VP for Oilfield Services and Equipment Amerino Gatti. "By deploying flexible pipe systems that last for decades, Petrobras can more efficiently unlock the vital natural resources that power the region, while also safely returning CO2 deep underground."

data.title: Star equity highlights $17.2M backlog growth and energy services expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Star Equity Holdings (STRR) Q4 2024

Management View

  • Revenue for the quarter increased by 21.1% to $17.1 million, driven by acquisitions of Timber Technologies and Big Lake Lumber. Full-year revenue reached $53.4 million, a 16.5% year-over-year growth.
  • Gross profit for Q4 rose 55.3% to $4.5 million due to the high-margin contribution from Timber Technologies. However, full-year gross profit declined 7.2% due to a one-time purchase price accounting adjustment and lower utilization in KBS and EBGL businesses.
  • CEO Richard Coleman highlighted that the Building Solutions division experienced a rebound in the second half of 2024, supported by delayed projects receiving final approvals. Backlog at year-end was $17.2 million and has increased year-to-date.
  • The company announced the acquisition of Alliance Drilling Tools (ADT), forming the Energy Services division. ADT generated $10.5 million in revenue for 2024, with a 48% gross margin and $2.4 million in adjusted EBITDA. This acquisition diversifies Star Equity’s operating portfolio.
  • CFO David Noble noted that SG&A expenses increased by $1 million in Q4 primarily due to acquisitions. The net loss from continuing operations for Q4 was $2.5 million, contrasting with a $1.8 million net income in Q4 2023.

Outlook

  • Management expressed optimism for a strong 2025, citing increased demand and new project signings within the Building Solutions division.
  • The company plans to leverage structural tailwinds in factory-built construction and expand its Energy Services division through strategic acquisitions and organic growth opportunities.

Financial Results

  • Consolidated gross profit for Q4 2024 was $4.4 million, a 55.9% increase year-over-year, driven by higher revenues and improved gross margins in the Building Solutions division.
  • Adjusted EBITDA from continuing operations for Q4 was $1.1 million, compared to a negative $0.1 million in Q4 2023. The Building Solutions division contributed $2.3 million to adjusted EBITDA in Q4.
  • Cash flow from operations in Q4 was an outflow of $1.5 million, attributed to increased working capital needs. The cash balance, including restricted cash, stood at $5.6 million as of December 31, 2024.
  • Debt increased to $11.3 million from $2.0 million at the end of 2023, primarily due to financing for the Timber Technologies acquisition.

Q&A

  • Theodore O'Neill, Litchfield Hills Research: Asked about the margin improvement in Building Solutions and its sustainability. CEO Richard Coleman attributed it to increased revenues and spreading fixed costs across a larger revenue base.
  • Tate Sullivan, Maxim Group: Inquired about ADT’s customer mix and growth potential. CEO Coleman emphasized ADT’s diverse client base and minimal credit risk, supported by household names in the energy sector.
  • An unnamed private investor raised concerns about the stock's undervaluation and the potential for selling the company. Executive Chairman Jeffrey Eberwein stated that all options to maximize shareholder value are under consideration.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed mixed concerns about the company’s challenges, particularly around impairments and stock valuation, while recognizing improvements in operational performance and strategic initiatives.
  • Management displayed confidence in their growth strategy and acquisitions, though there was a cautious tone regarding external risks like tariffs and macroeconomic uncertainties.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue increased sequentially, driven by contributions from Timber Technologies and Big Lake Lumber acquisitions.
  • Gross profit showed strong improvement in Q4 due to higher margins and increased project activity, compared to the first half of 2024 where demand softness prevailed.
  • Management’s tone in Q4 was more optimistic compared to Q3, supported by a stronger backlog and the establishment of the Energy Services division.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted potential risks from fiscal policy changes, including tariffs on Canadian lumber, which could impact input costs and demand.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the sustainability of recent margin improvements and the impact of rising SG&A expenses on profitability.
  • The company cited strategies such as hedging and contract adjustments to mitigate input cost volatility.

Final Takeaway

Star Equity Holdings demonstrated a significant rebound in Q4 2024, supported by strategic acquisitions, an expanding backlog, and improved margins in its Building Solutions division. The formation of the Energy Services division through the acquisition of Alliance Drilling Tools positions the company for diversified growth. However, risks related to fiscal policies, cost controls, and ongoing macroeconomic uncertainties remain key areas of focus for management and investors.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: U.S. stocks under pressure as investors gear up for $4.5T options test
data.description:
Wall Street on Thursday inched down, as traders expressed some caution a day ahead of a major quarterly options expiration event.

Some positive economic data on the housing market helped overcome a wobbly start, but the three major averages were unable to hold on to those gains. After some mixed trading, they had appeared to turn decidedly lower.  

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last -0.54% at 5,644.62 points in late afternoon trade, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.72% at 17,622.68 points. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) was -0.29% at 41,844.83 points.

Friday marks a "triple witching" day - an occasion on which stock options, stock index futures and stock index options are set to expire together. Citi estimates about $4.5T of contracts will expire.

Earlier, shortly after the opening bell, data from the National Association of Realtors showed total U.S. existing home sales rising 4.2% from January to 4.26M in February, easily beating the estimate of 3.95M.

"Don’t get too excited over the jump in existing home sales (closed contracts). Pending home sales, which represent signed contracts, recently fell to fresh lows. This implies closings soften into Q2," Renaissance Macro Research cautioned on X (formerly Twitter).

The economic calendar also had the Conference Board's monthly leading indicator index update. Long seen as a recession indicator, the index declined further in February. 

Market participants overnight tempered some of the euphoria sparked by Fed chair Jerome Powell calling tariff-induced inflation "transitory" on Wednesday.

"There was a quiet start to trading on Thursday following last night’s Fed meeting. But there was a sudden ‘risk off’ move mid-morning which saw global stock indices slump, alongside gold, and a rush into the safe-haven of U.S. Treasury bonds," David Morrison, senior market analyst at Trade Nation, said. 

"There was no obvious trigger for the move. Perhaps it simply indicates skittishness amongst traders. There’s currently a high level of uncertainty prevalent amongst market participants due to all the complex geopolitical factors currently at play, together with the mercurial nature of the Trump administration," he added.

A rally in bonds triggered by the Fed extended to Thursday, sending U.S. Treasury yields lower. The 10-year yield (US10Y) was down slightly to 4.24%, while the shorter-end 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 2 basis points to 3.97%. 

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.  

Turning to active stocks, Accenture (ACN) was the top percentage loser on the S&P 500 (SP500), -7.4%, after the IT and consulting services provider said Elon Musk's push for government efficiency had led to a negative impact on sales and revenue.

data.description:
